visual_checks: Check if the page auto-redirects to another page without user action
Look for content that updates or changes automatically without the user requesting it
Check if new windows or tabs open without the user clicking a link or button
Look for forms that auto-submit when a selection is made (e.g., dropdown changes triggering navigation)
Check if carousels or slideshows advance automatically without user control
Verify that any automatic context changes can be disabled or are initiated only on user request
pass_conditions: Context changes occur only when the user explicitly requests them (button click, link activation, form submission)
Auto-updating content has a mechanism to pause, stop, or disable automatic changes
No new windows or popups open unless the user clicks a control that clearly indicates it will open a new window
Form selections do not trigger automatic navigation or submission — a submit button is required
Carousels and slideshows only advance when the user activates next/previous controls or autoplay can be disabled
fail_conditions: The page auto-redirects to a different URL after a timed delay without user action
Selecting an option in a dropdown automatically navigates to a new page without a Go button
New browser windows or tabs open automatically without user-initiated action
Content on the page changes automatically (auto-refresh) with no way to disable it
A carousel advances slides automatically with no pause or stop mechanism
na_conditions: Page is entirely static with no dynamic content, automatic updates, or context-changing interactions
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Dynamic behavior and context changes could not be observed in the testing environment
common_mistakes: Using onChange events on select elements to auto-navigate instead of requiring a submit action
Auto-advancing carousels or news tickers with no visible pause control
Meta refresh tags that redirect the page after a delay
Opening links in new windows via target='_blank' without informing the user
Live regions that update content automatically without a mechanism for the user to control updates

examples.pass: A category filter dropdown is accompanied by a 'Go' button — selecting a category does nothing until the user clicks the button, and a carousel has visible play/pause controls defaulting to paused
examples.fail: A language selector dropdown automatically redirects the user to a different page version when an option is selected, and a news carousel auto-advances every 3 seconds with no pause control
examples.partial: Most interactive elements require explicit user action, but one navigation dropdown on the site auto-submits on change, redirecting the user without clicking a button

plain_meaning: Changes of context (new windows, page navigation, major layout changes, form submission) must only happen when the user explicitly asks for them, OR the user must be able to turn off automatic context changes. Auto-refreshing pages, auto-opening new windows, and automatic redirects all fall under this rule.
scope_applies_to: Automatic page refreshes that reload or redirect
Pop-up windows that open without user action
Content that reloads or navigates on a schedule
Auto-play behaviors that open new contexts
Any mechanism where a context change happens without user request
scope_does_not_apply_to: Changes triggered directly by user click, keypress, or explicit gesture
Popovers, tooltips, and in-page expansions (not context changes)
Timed warnings that do not themselves change context
Pages with no automatic behavior at all
borderline_calibration: This is Level AAA and often Not Applicable.
If an automatic context change exists AND a mechanism to turn it off is available, PASS.
Only flag when automatic context change is observed AND no opt-out is offered.
Prefer Not Enough Evidence if no timed behavior was captured.
